what_it_does: This file is the top-level README and entry point for the 'Claude Code Extended Interconnection' subsystem: it states the problem being solved, lists the components, maps the documents in the directory, summarizes what has been proven, and states the one hard operational constraint.
observations: Built and 'proven by use' on 2026-06-14 with Tim watching.
The asset being managed is the live context of each Claude Code conversation.
Core mechanism is a channel that injects messages into a live session and pushes replies back.
There is an MCP tool named mcp__company__cc_channel that is the agent surface.
Components include Channels, cc_channels core, Supervisor routes, MCP tool, Session Fabric, Mirror-Registry, and @xsession loadout.
The directory contains ARCHITECTURE.md, REFERENCE.md, and ROADMAP.md.
Hard constraint: channels only work in interactive sessions, not headless claude -p.
Operational how-tos are stored as skills, while this directory holds architecture and reference.
standouts: Live injection and reply-routing between actual Claude Code sessions, not fresh stand-ins.
A single chat plus the Company MCP can drive a fleet of sessions.
A session can be cloned and relaunched at a specific point in time.
Channels are explicitly limited to interactive sessions.
conventions: Markdown README with H1 title and linked sibling docs.
Uses a table to map 'Piece | What it does | Where'.
Labels proven status with a date and evidence paths.
Uses bold for key terms and file paths.
concerns: The proven date 2026-06-14 is in the future relative to current knowledge, so it reads as planned/aspirational rather than historical.
Claims evidence in external files (build-prep/CROSS-SESSION-CHANNELS-PROVEN.md, .data/channels/_mail.jsonl) are referenced but not shown.
Heavy jargon and product-specific terms (Company MCP, R3.4, @xsession, Mirror-Registry) are used without in-file definitions.
'next' voice features are listed but not confirmed built.
notes: This is the orientation page for a system that turns many live Claude Code sessions into a messageable, group-chat-capable fleet driven from one chat.
questions: What exactly is the 'Company MCP' and how is it configured?
How does the channel technically inject a message into a live Claude Code conversation?
What does R3.4 mean in the Session Fabric context?
How does the Mirror-Registry perform binary-discovered introspection?
What is an '@xsession loadout' and which services in ops/services.json are involved?
What is a 'skill' in this repository and how does it relate to the README?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is an overview README that introduces the system and points readers to deeper architecture, reference, and roadmap documents.
apparent_themes: Fleet orchestration of AI coding sessions
Preservation and reuse of live conversation context
Human-in-the-loop control via a single chat interface
Introspection and registration of tool/capability surfaces
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a coordinated fabric out of many independent Claude Code sessions so an operator can discover, message, broadcast, clone, and relaunch any session from one place, eventually including voice interaction.
novelty: It treats each live Claude Code conversation's context as a first-class asset and proposes injecting messages into actual interactive sessions with reply pushback, group threads, and point-in-time materialization rather than starting fresh stand-ins.
contribution: Provides a high-level map and entry point for the interconnection subsystem, names each component and its location, links to the architecture, reference, and roadmap documents, records claimed proven capabilities and evidence paths, and states the interactive-only constraint that governs deployment.
summary_for_embedding: README overview for a system that connects live Claude Code sessions so they can be discovered, messaged, broadcasted, cloned, and relaunched from a single chat via an MCP tool and a channel/supervisor fabric.
intention_for_embedding: To orient readers and operators to the Claude Code Extended Interconnection system, list its pieces and documents, summarize proven behavior, and enforce the interactive-session constraint.
